prompt:
## Intent

Verify status indicator

## Desired Outcome

Verify the visual and functional state of the status indicator component.

## Repo Context

- Intent Driven Development POC workspace
- Intent Driven Development POC: Current workspace source for Intent Studio, surface library evidence flows, and built-in surface library screenshots.
- intent-poc-app verification note: Stateful UI bugs should declare and verify the requested UI state before screenshot evidence is trusted.
- intent-poc-app verification note: Prefer reusable state activation contracts over prompt-specific routing or one-off checkpoint logic.
- intent-poc-app verification note: Theme-sensitive UI bugs require paired light-mode and dark-mode screenshot evidence; do not accept a dark-mode-only capture set.

## Source Scope

- intent-poc-app
- Selected source: intent-poc-app. Prompt-matched captures: primitive-status-indicator.
- Likely owning surface: Shared Source (high confidence).

## Adaptive Boundaries

- Verify status indicator component behavior
- Ensure stateful UI verification before screenshot capture
- Keep this draft inside intent-poc-app until the owning surface is confirmed.

## Minimum Success

- Successful rendering of status indicator
- Paired light and dark mode screenshot evidence
- Verify the visual and functional state of the status indicator component.
- The draft keeps the change centered on Shared Source instead of the whole repo.
- The final plan reuses existing verification around primitive-status-indicator.

## Baseline

- Current status indicator implementation in intent-poc-app
- Capture the current baseline in Shared Source before changing behavior.
- Stay inside the prompt-matched capture scope: primitive-status-indicator.

## Verification Obligations

- Use theme-mode activation contract
- Provide light and dark mode screenshots
- Start from existing capture coverage: primitive-status-indicator.

## Raw Intent

Verify status indicator

normalizedIntent.rawPrompt:
Verify status indicator

Desired outcome: Verify the visual and functional state of the status indicator component.

codeSurface.label: Shared Source
codeSurface.rationale: Prompt normalization identified Shared Source as the most likely code surface within intent-poc-app.

businessIntent.statement: Verify the visual and functional state of the status indicator component in the intent-poc-app.
businessIntent.desiredOutcome: Ensure the status indicator component renders correctly across light and dark themes and maintains consistent visual styling.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The status indicator component is rendered in the intent-poc-app.
Visual regression tests confirm the component appearance in both light and dark modes.
The component state is verified using the primitive-status-indicator capture path.

scenarios.title: Verify status indicator visual consistency
scenarios.goal: Validate the status indicator component appearance in both light and dark themes.
given: The status indicator component is accessible at /library/primitive-status-indicator.
when: The application is set to light mode.
A screenshot of the status indicator is captured.
The application is set to dark mode using the dark query parameter.
then: The light mode screenshot matches the baseline.
The dark mode screenshot matches the baseline.
Both screenshots are packaged for review.

workItems.title: Verify status indicator visual consistency
workItems.description: Validate the status indicator component appearance in both light and dark themes.
workItems.userVisibleOutcome: The status indicator component renders correctly across light and dark themes and maintains consistent visual styling.
workItems.verification: Visual regression tests confirm the component appearance in both light and dark modes using the primitive-status-indicator capture path.

specs.testName: Verify status indicator in light and dark modes

checkpoints.label: Navigate to status indicator in light mode
checkpoints.assertion: The page is loaded in light mode.

requiredUiStates.label: Theme mode
requiredUiStates.description: The demo app supports light and dark theme states that materially change contrast, tokens, and screenshot evidence.

notes: The demo app now honors the dark query parameter across Studio and library routes for deterministic verification.
The library surface toggle remains available for manual verification flows.
Intent Studio keeps an explicit toggle for interactive review during manual sessions.
Stateful UI bugs should declare and verify the requested UI state before screenshot evidence is trusted.
Prefer reusable state activation contracts over prompt-specific routing or one-off checkpoint logic.
Theme-sensitive UI bugs require paired light-mode and dark-mode screenshot evidence; do not accept a dark-mode-only capture set.
Verify the active theme state before trusting screenshot comparisons.
Prefer the shared dark query parameter during generated verification so screenshots start in the requested theme.
Dark-theme verification should keep a light-mode reference screenshot alongside the dark-mode target evidence.

destinations.label: Controller artifacts
destinations.reason: Local evidence bundles are always written by the controller.
details: Stores plan, manifests, logs, captures, and summaries on disk.

destinations.label: Source workspace publication
destinations.reason: Workspace publishing is disabled until artifacts.storageMode is set to 'both'.
details: Useful when evidence should live beside the source repository.

tools.label: BDD planning
tools.reason: The system converts the raw prompt into reviewable acceptance criteria and scenarios during the full reviewed plan pass.
details: Deterministic by default, with optional Gemini refinement when configured.

tools.label: Playwright TDD generation
tools.reason: Applicable sources now produce Playwright-first executable test plans and checkpoint screenshots.
details: Generated specs are intended for checked-in repo storage with overwrite semantics.

tools.label: Visual verification
tools.reason: Applicable sources currently expose visual checkpoints and capture definitions.
details: Playwright screenshots remain one execution tool rather than the top-level product model.

tools.label: Environment deployment
tools.reason: A generic local or Kubernetes deployment stage is planned but not yet wired into the runner.
details: This will become the shared path for local, minikube, and k3s-backed execution.

tools.label: Implementation loop
tools.reason: The runner can execute bounded implementation attempts against the prepared source workspace.
details: QA failures can feed directly back into the next bounded implementation attempt.

tools.label: QA verification
tools.reason: The runner can now execute a bounded QA bundle before evidence capture completes the lane.
details: The default QA bundle runs typecheck, the deterministic code test suite, and generated Playwright specs when a source enables them.

tools.label: Evidence reporting
tools.reason: Every run produces summaries and machine-readable manifests.
details: Reports become distribution inputs for downstream review and publishing surfaces.

stages.label: Prompt Interpretation
stages.description: Bound the raw prompt to intent type, source scope, and capture hints before planning.

stages.label: Linear Scoping
stages.description: Shape the work into resumable Linear-owned lanes before execution planning expands.

stages.label: BDD Planning

stages.label: TDD Planning
stages.description: Translate the accepted scenarios into Playwright-first executable test artifacts.

stages.label: Implementation
stages.description: Apply the planned changes against the scoped source workspace.
warnings: Implementation stage configuration is recorded and executes during source runs before QA verification.

stages.label: QA Verification
stages.description: Verify implementation output, tests, and evidence before completion or retry.
warnings: QA verification configuration is recorded and executes during source runs after implementation completes.
